text: The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position on the operating table, with the buttocks taped aside to provide clear access to the anal region for the surgical procedure.
The surgeon injects normal saline into the external fistula opening, observing for any flow that would confirm the presence of an internal opening.
The surgeon probes the intersphincteric fistula tract, visually assessing its path and depth.
The surgeon performs a fistulotomy on the intersphincteric fistula, making an incision along the fistula tract to open it.
The surgeon performs curettage on the opened fistula tract, removing debris and granulation tissue from the exposed channel.
The surgeon inserts a hemostatic sponge into the anal canal, likely to control bleeding or provide support within the surgical site.
